data.content_ref: inline:contradicts: The winter storm surge hypothesis (8f32aa39316b) requires a period of extreme storminess to salinize coastal soils, while the cooler-wetter climate shift hypothesis (a440e9c75cc3) posits a hydroclimatic regime dominated by gradual flood-inducing precipitation. These mechanisms are meteorologically incompatible for the same narrow 1220–1150 BCE window, as the persistent atmospheric instability driving repeated severe cyclonic storm surges is antithetical to the stable, cooler-wetter pattern described. REFUTED IF it can be shown that the proposed cooler-wetter regime is consistent with, or a direct driver of, an increased frequency of winter storm surges in the Eastern Mediterranean.
